title: CIAS
description: Circuit Agnostic Structure (CIAS) — a reusable circuit BRICK: a list of components and how they connect, plus the external terminals (ports) through which the brick wires to other bricks. The SPICE analogy is a .subckt: a definition with a port (node) list and internal elements, NOT runnable by itself. A CIAS carries NO usage meaning — no stage, no role, no 'why', no stimulus, no analysis. That all lives in TAS, where each stage instantiates one CIAS and adds the usage. Names (component/port/connection) are LOCAL to the brick; a TAS that instantiates the brick references its terminals stage-qualified, exactly as a SPICE parent references a subckt instance's nodes. A brick has no magnetic-coupling edges: a coupled inductor or transformer is a single multi-winding PEAS component, whose coupling lives inside its own (MAS) model.

name.description: Brick type name, unique in a brick library (e.g. 'half-bridge', 'llc-tank', 'diode-bridge'). Required: it is the lookup key when a stage references a brick by URI (e.g. 'TAS/data/circuits.ndjson?name=half-bridge').

ports.description: External terminals of the brick — its connection studs. A TAS stage types and wires these. A net is exposed at a terminal by including a portEndpoint that names it.

port.title: port
port.description: One external terminal of the brick. Name is unique within the brick; it is the handle a TAS uses to type and wire this terminal.

name.description: Terminal name, unique within this brick (e.g. 'dc+', 'sw', 'out').

description.description: What this terminal is, in brick-local terms.

component.title: component
component.description: One placed part in the brick: a reference designator (local to the brick) plus the part itself — an inline PEAS document, or a URI string into a part-data file.

name.description: Reference designator unique within THIS brick (e.g. 'Qh', 'Lr', 'C_in'). Connection endpoints refer to it by this name.

data.description: The component itself: an inline PEAS document, or a URI string into a part-data file (e.g. 'TAS/data/mosfets.ndjson?partNumber=C3M0032120K').

connection.title: connection
connection.description: An internal electrical net shared by 2+ endpoints. A net that includes a portEndpoint is exposed at that brick terminal. (Bricks have only electrical nets — no magnetic-coupling edges; coupling lives inside a multi-winding PEAS component.)

name.description: Net name, unique within this brick.

endpoint.title: endpoint
endpoint.$comment: An endpoint of a net: either a specific pin of a component, or one of the brick's external terminals (which exposes the net there).

pinEndpoint.title: pinEndpoint
pinEndpoint.description: An endpoint that lands on a specific pin of a component in this brick.

component.description: Refers to a component.name in this brick.

portEndpoint.title: portEndpoint
portEndpoint.description: An endpoint that lands on one of this brick's external terminals, exposing the net there.

port.description: Refers to a port.name declared in this brick.
